reasoning:
The comparison price is the market close on Friday, February 27, 2026, which was 312.04. The stock has experienced a massive, rapid surge in late February from ~296 to 312, leaving it highly vulnerable to short-term mean reversion, although upward momentum is currently strong. 

For the first resolution date (March 8), the most recent market close will be Friday, March 6. This avoids a critical corporate event: CME has declared a combined $7.45 per share dividend (a $6.15 annual variable dividend plus a $1.30 regular Q1 dividend) for shareholders of record on Tuesday, March 10, 2026. Because March 6 is prior to the ex-dividend date, the March 8 resolution will not suffer the dividend price drop, making the probability a near coin-flip with a slight penalty for recent runaway momentum (0.46). 

For the second resolution date (March 31), the stock will have gone ex-dividend, mechanically stripping approximately $7.45 (or 2.4% of its value) from its share price. To clear the 312.04 hurdle on March 31, the stock will essentially need to appreciate by an additional $7.45 to overcome this penalty. Despite having higher variance (uncertainty) over the 1-month horizon, this deterministic $7.45 drag severely lowers the expected value of the stock, decisively pulling the likelihood of resolving YES down to roughly 0.34.
